name: Nid-Hca Albany (Fletcher)
description_short: HUD-approved housing counseling agency offering free guidance on homeownership, financial stability, and housing solutions through certified counselors and educational workshops.
description_long: NID Housing Counseling Agency is a national HUD-approved intermediary organization that functions as a dynamic network of advocacy groups, housing counselors, real estate professionals, community organizations, and faith-based groups. The organization has maintained operations even during government shutdowns, demonstrating commitment to serving Americans in need. Their mission centers on empowering individuals and communities through comprehensive housing solutions while fostering sustainable homeownership and resilient neighborhoods. NID operates as both a direct counseling provider and a training/advocacy network that addresses systemic housing disparities. They offer completely free services to all clients, responding to inquiries within 48 hours and providing personalized guidance tailored to individual circumstances. The agency has documented success stories, including clients who received $80,000 in down payment assistance through their programs and first-time homebuyers who closed on homes after working with NID counselors over multi-year periods. Their approach emphasizes not just transactional assistance but long-term financial stability and community resilience. A notable strength is their role as a HUD-Approved Intermediary, which carries regulatory oversight and quality standards. However, the website provides limited specific details about which locations they serve beyond references to Atlanta and Albany. The organization also functions as a training provider for other housing counselors, suggesting they operate at both direct service and systemic levels. One consideration is that as a network rather than a single entity, service quality and availability may vary by location.
pros: HUD-approved intermediary status provides regulatory credibility and oversight
All services are completely free with no hidden fees or charges
Responds to service requests within 48 hours
Offers down payment assistance program connections (documented $80,000 awards in their case studies)
Provides HUD-certified counselor access for personalized guidance
Operates as a comprehensive network including faith-based and community organizations
Hosts free workshops, webinars, and training sessions for multiple audiences
cons: Website lacks specific service availability details for the Albany (Fletcher) location
Limited information about typical counseling timelines or commitment requirements
No details on which specific down payment assistance programs they can access by location
Network structure may result in inconsistent service quality across different branches
No information about counseling modality (phone, in-person, online) or appointment availability
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking pre-purchase education and down payment assistance pathways
Individuals working toward financial stability and homeownership over multi-year periods
Low-to-moderate income households needing free guidance without predatory lending alternatives
diagnosis: NID Housing Counseling Agency is best for first-time homebuyers and individuals seeking free, non-predatory guidance on homeownership and financial stability from a HUD-certified organization. The primary caveat is that service availability and specific program access (particularly down payment assistance) vary by location, and the website provides limited transparency about what services are currently available in the Albany (Fletcher) office specifically.
services: Free housing counseling from HUD-certified counselors
Homebuyer workshops (documented as program requirements in Atlanta)
Down payment assistance program referrals and support
Housing counselor training and certification programs
Advocacy support for housing access and equity issues
HBCU/MSI partnership programs for affordable student housing
Community development resources and best practices guidance
Personalized case management and document portal access
Support for accessing quality mortgage products
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 230 S Jackson St Ste 154, Albany, GA 31701
